recapPrompt.prompt: When binary search is not looking for an exact match, what might it be looking for instead?

choices.label: A position or smallest valid answer
choices.feedback: Correct. Binary search can find where something belongs or the best answer that still works.

choices.label: A random middle value
choices.feedback: Not this one. The search still follows a clear goal.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why checking the middle is a smart move.
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: Checking the middle is smart because...

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the binary-search clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for each kind of binary-search job.
hints: Exact search stops when the middle matches.
Insert-position search returns where left lands.
Duplicate-position search saves an answer and keeps going.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main binary-search clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck how exact search, insert position, and duplicate search differ.

blanks.label: Main clue for first or last occurrence
